chat.author: Jan-Frederik Rieckers
chat.text: For remote: Question was about if recharter should be discussed now

chat.author: Heikki Vatiainen
chat.text: RadSec goes back to late 2005. It has long history

chat.author: Heikki Vatiainen
chat.text: So one way to describe CHAP is to say that it sends your password almost in plaintext and forces you to store your passwords in cleartext.

chat.author: Heikki Vatiainen
chat.text: Wasn't it also so that we'd need to finish some of the work before considering QUIC?

chat.author: Alexander Clouter
chat.text: @fabian, I am seeing it more as a 'sink' for the data, if that service then demultiplexes and forwards different event streams elesewhere, that is just an implementation

chat.author: Alexander Clouter
chat.text: as QUIC even does multicast (as well as anycast) I can see a receiver just ignoring the stuff it does not care about

chat.author: Alan DeKok
chat.text: @heikki yes.  the quic doc is for now just "FYI, people are thinking of this".  There's no plans on bringing this to RADEXT for additional work

chat.author: Christopher Inacio
chat.text:
Jan-Frederik Rieckers said:

For remote: Question was about if recharter should be discussed now

the new charter is with the secretariat having passed through the IESG.

chat.author: Heikki Vatiainen
chat.text: @alan, agree on your comment the OPSAWG draft. I'll clarify that was thinking of QUIC as Radius transport

chat.author: Alan DeKok
chat.text: I think there's no interest in RADIUS over QUIC right now.  we can likely review that in a 6-12 months

chat.author: Michael Sym
chat.text: there's a need in the wifi roaming industry to standardize the reporting of network metrics over RADIUS.  I think it's important to publish as a draft for better adoption from AP/WLC vendors.

chat.author: Heikki Vatiainen
chat.text: Yep, QUIC transport was for later.

chat.author: Alan DeKok
chat.text: if there's a need for a standard, then these attributes could be in the IANA space, instead of in the WBA space?

chat.author: Alan DeKok
chat.text: it depends on what people want. there is no requirement for them to be in the IETF / IANA space

chat.author: Michael Sym
chat.text: I'd support them in the IETF / IANA space

chat.author: Heikki Vatiainen
chat.text: Would this be a standards track document? How or what decides that?

chat.author: Alan DeKok
chat.text: the author can ask it to be made a WG document.  the WG can decide what to do

chat.author: Alan DeKok
chat.text: thanks!

chat.author: Alexander Clouter
chat.text: cheers

chat.author: Joey Padden
chat.text: thanks all

chat.author: Michael Sym
chat.text: thanks everyone!

chat.author: Heikki Vatiainen
chat.text: Thanks!
